title: Grassy Russian Mentaiko Pilaf
description: A hearty dairy-free-friendly Russian-inspired pilaf featuring the rich flavors of mentaiko and fresh herbs, perfect for a hangover cure.
instructions: Heat ghee in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add rice and cook, stirring occasionally, until lightly toasted, about 5 minutes.
Add 300ml of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the water has been absorbed and the rice is cooked.
Flake in mentaiko and stir to combine.
Stir in chopped herbs (parsley, dill, and chives).
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the pilaf to a serving dish and drizzle with sesame oil, if desired.
Garnish with additional herbs, if desired.
Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of turmeric, if desired.
Dehydrate the pilaf for 10-15 minutes to enhance the flavors and textures, if desired.
